topic.what: Unsupervised ICS anomaly diagnosis framework combining sensor-actuator context-conditioned detection with LLM-grounded contextual explanations
topic.how: SCDT learns normal behavioral envelopes via multi-stage S-AC clustering and uses Primary/Secondary LLMs to translate detector evidence into actionable diagnostic hypotheses
topic.why_matters: Bridges the detection-diagnosis gap by providing root cause analysis beyond opaque anomaly scores; enables operators to initiate timely response without relying on scarce labeled attack data

characteristics.what_it_measures: Overall detection accuracy balancing precision and recall
characteristics.vs_baseline: Best on SWaT, second-best on WADI and HAI
characteristics.evidence: Table 3 comparison with PCA, KNN, OCSVM, AE, DAGMM, LSTM-VAE, MAD-GAN, USAD, GDN, FuSAGNet
characteristics.context: F1-score jointly reflects false positives and false negatives across three public ICS benchmarks

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of detected anomalies that are true positives
characteristics.vs_baseline: Top-tier precision across all three datasets
characteristics.context: SCDT maintains top-tier precision indicating fewer false alarms

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of actual anomalies correctly detected
characteristics.vs_baseline: Best on SWaT, moderate on WADI and HAI
characteristics.context: Recall lower on WADI/HAI due to actuator-context partitioning creating sparse S-AC contexts

characteristics.vs_baseline: Fast deterministic screening with envelope membership and robust distance checks
characteristics.evidence: Abstract and Section 3.5 describe lightweight online screening
characteristics.context: Framework designed for low-latency warning and practical deployment

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ability to provide actionable diagnostic hypotheses
characteristics.vs_baseline: Qualitative analysis shows meaningful operational relationships captured
characteristics.evidence: Figure 4 case study demonstrates LLM-generated diagnostic narratives identifying actuator-related causes
characteristics.context: Secondary LLM identifies which actuator-sensor expectation is most inconsistent with observation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Handling of ambiguous or unseen actuator combinations
characteristics.value: explicit ambiguity marking with LLM arbitration
characteristics.vs_baseline: Treats low-confidence context matching as ambiguous
characteristics.evidence: Section 3.5 describes fallback retrieval via S-A index and LLM arbitration mode
characteristics.context: Robust handling of unseen combinations preserves safety

good_for: unsupervised anomaly detection without labeled attack data
interpretable diagnosis with root cause analysis
real-time ICS monitoring with low-latency alerts
context-aware detection under stable actuator configurations
not_for: Settings with extensive labeled attack data where supervised methods excel
Datasets with wide operating ranges causing S-AC context fragmentation
Highly dynamic actuator configurations with sparse per-context samples
requires: GPU acceleration for UMAP and HDBSCAN clustering
LLM integration capability (GPT-5-mini used in evaluation)

novelty: Formulates ICS anomaly diagnosis around sensor-actuator context for context-conditioned monitoring aligned with closed-loop control
Introduces LLM-based diagnostic layer converting detector evidence into actionable grounded hypotheses
Presents robust handling of ambiguous contexts via explicit low-confidence marking and S-A index fallback
Provides modular reasoning module attachable to alternative detectors for actuator-aware contextual explanations
open_problems: Actuator-context partitioning creates sparse S-AC contexts reducing detection recall on diverse operating datasets
Cross-segment generalization needs improvement for datasets like HAI with multi-process interactions
Fusion with stronger detection backbones not explored despite modular design
Automatic adaptation to plant configuration changes requires further investigation
